description: Name of the country where the data was recorded.
The calendar year of the data recording (2000-2015).
Development status of the country (e.g., 'Developed' or 'Developing').
TARGET VARIABLE: The average life expectancy in age (years).
Probability of dying between 15 and 60 years of age (per 1000 population).
Number of infant deaths per 1000 population.
Alcohol consumption per capita for ages 15+ (in liters of pure alcohol).
Expenditure on health as a percentage of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) per capita.
Hepatitis B (HepB) immunization coverage among 1-year-olds (percentage).
Number of reported measles cases per 1000 population.
Average Body Mass Index of the entire population.
Number of deaths of children under five years old per 1000 population.
Polio (Pol3) immunization coverage among 1-year-olds (percentage).
General government expenditure on health as a percentage of total government expenditure.
Diphtheria tetanus toxoid and pertussis (DTP3) immunization coverage among 1-year-olds (percentage).
Deaths per 1000 live births due to HIV/AIDS (0-4 years).
Gross Domestic Product per capita (in USD).
Total population of the country.
Prevalence of thinness among children and adolescents aged 10 to 19 (percentage).
Prevalence of thinness among children aged 5 to 9 (percentage).
Human Development Index in terms of income composition of resources (index ranging from 0 to 1).
Average number of years of schooling.
